import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.P1BaseCase
import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.Cokernel

/-!
# H¹ finiteness of a curve via a finite map to ℙ¹ (node `N11`, the adelic keystone)

This file is part of the **adelic Riemann–Roch lane** (see the lane design document).
It proves the lane's primary keystone: for a curve `C` over a field `k` admitting a
finite morphism `π : C ⟶ Y` to a target carrying *Laurent chart data* (the abstract
form of the standard two-chart structure of `ℙ¹`), the two-cover Čech cokernel
`Ȟ¹ = Γ(U₀ ∩ U₁, 𝒪_C) / (Γ(U₀, 𝒪_C) + Γ(U₁, 𝒪_C))` of the structure sheaf on the
pulled-back cover `U_i = π⁻¹V_i` is a **finite-dimensional `k`-vector space**.

## The mathematical route (Weil/Stichtenoth two-lattice argument)

Fix the standard cover `ℙ¹ = V₀ ∪ V₁` with coordinate `x` on `V₀` and `y = x⁻¹`
on `V₁`, so `V₀ ∩ V₁ = D(x) = D(y)` and `Γ(V₀) = k[x]`, `Γ(V₁) = k[y]`,
`Γ(V₀ ∩ V₁) = k[x, x⁻¹]`.  Pull back along the finite `π`:

* the preimages `U_i = π⁻¹V_i` and `U₀ ∩ U₁` are affine (finite morphisms are
  affine), and `A_i := Γ(U_i, 𝒪_C)` is a **finite module** over the chart ring
  `Γ(V_i)` (`IsFinite.finite_app`, the integral-closure finiteness);
* `U₀ ∩ U₁` is the basic open of `t := π^♯x` inside `U₀` (and of `u := π^♯y`
  inside `U₁`), so `M := Γ(U₀ ∩ U₁, 𝒪_C)` is the **localization** `(A₀)_t = (A₁)_u`
  (`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`); in particular every `m ∈ M`
  *extends*: `t^n·m` comes from `A₀` and `u^n·m` comes from `A₁` for `n ≫ 0`;
* choosing finitely many `Γ(V₀)`-module generators `g₁, …, g_r` of `A₀` and using
  that `Γ(V₀)` is the `k`-span of the powers of `x` (the ℙ¹ chart structure,
  mirroring the Laurent split of `P1BaseCase`), `M` is the `k`-span of the
  doubly-infinite ladder `{t^j · ρ(g_i) : j ∈ ℤ}`;
* rungs with `j ≥ 0` lie in the image of `A₀`; by the extension property each
  generator has all rungs `j ≤ -N_i` in the image of `A₁`; the **finitely many**
  middle rungs `{t^{-j} · ρ(g_i) : 0 < j < N_i}` span the cokernel.

This is the algebraic incarnation of the elementary-divisor/two-lattice proof that
`H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` is finite-dimensional (Stichtenoth, *Algebraic Function Fields and
Codes* I.4–I.5; the design document node `N11`), with the ℙ¹ base case
`k[x,x⁻¹] = k[x] + k[x⁻¹]` of `P1BaseCase.lean` as organizing model — here the
split enters through the field `span_pow_x` of the chart data.

## Main statements

* `Adelic.module_finite_quotient_of_laurent_pair` — **the abstract two-lattice
  core**: in a commutative `k`-algebra `M` with `t * u = 1`, if `N₀` is a
  `t`-stable and `N₁` a `u`-stable `k`-submodule, `s ⊆ N₀` a finite set whose
  `t`-`u`-ladder spans `M`, and every element of `s` lands in `N₁` after enough
  multiplications by `u`, then `M ⧸ (N₀ ⊔ N₁)` is a finite `k`-module.
* `Adelic.exists_pow_mul_eq_res` — **the extension lemma**: on an affine open
  `U` with `W = D(f) ⊆ U`, every section over `W` multiplied by a high power of
  `f` extends to `U` (qcqs-free affine form, via
  `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`).
* `Adelic.LaurentChartData` — the bundled standard-two-chart structure on a
  `Spec k`-scheme: two affine opens covering it, coordinate sections `x`, `y`
  with `x·y = 1` on the overlap, overlap = basic open of either coordinate, and
  each chart ring spanned over `k` by the powers of its coordinate.
* `Adelic.LaurentChartData.pullbackSquare` — the pulled-back 2-affine cover
  `AffineCoverMVSquare` on the source of a finite morphism.
* `Adelic.LaurentChartData.module_finite_H1Cok` — **the keystone (node `N11`)**:
  `Module.Finite k (H1Cok (pullbackSquare) (toModuleKSheaf C))` for any finite
  `k`-morphism `π : C ⟶ Y` to a target with Laurent chart data.
* `Adelic.exists_affineCoverMVSquare_module_finite_H1Cok` — the consumable form
  under the `HasFiniteMapToP1` gate (plus the ℙ¹-chart-data gate
  `P1HasLaurentChartData`): *some* 2-affine cover of `C` has finite `Ȟ¹`.

## Gating

`HasFiniteMapToP1` (node `N9`, from `P1BaseCase.lean`) and the new
`P1HasLaurentChartData` (the computation of the standard chart rings of the
`Proj`-pullback model `ℙ(ULift (Fin 2); Spec k)`, i.e. `Γ(V₀) = k[x]` etc.) are
`HasPicScheme`-style gates: single-field `Prop` classes with no sorried
instances.  Everything else in this file is fully proved.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

/-! ## The abstract two-lattice core -/

section TwoLattice

variable {k : Type*} [Field k] {M : Type*} [CommRing M] [Algebra k M]

/-- Iterated stability: a `k`-submodule stable under multiplication by `t` is
stable under multiplication by every power `t ^ n`. -/
lemma pow_mul_mem {N : Submodule k M} {t : M} (h : ∀ a ∈ N, t * a ∈ N)
    (n : ℕ) {a : M} (ha : a ∈ N) : t ^ n * a ∈ N := by
  induction n with
  | zero => simpa using ha
  | succ n ih =>
    have hrw : t ^ (n + 1) * a = t * (t ^ n * a) := by ring
    rw [hrw]
    exact h _ ih

/-- **Ladder-spanning producer.** If every `m ∈ M` satisfies `t ^ n * m ∈
span k (positive ladder of s)` for some `n`, and `t * u = 1`, then the full
two-sided ladder `{t^j·a} ∪ {u^j·a}` (`a ∈ s`) spans `M` over `k`.  This is the
formal step turning the localization/extension property plus chart-spanning into
the spanning hypothesis of the two-lattice core. -/
theorem span_ladder_of_pow_mul_mem_span {t u : M} (htu : t * u = 1) (s : Set M)
    (H : ∀ m : M, ∃ n : ℕ,
      t ^ n * m ∈ Submodule.span k (⋃ j : ℕ, (fun z => t ^ j * z) '' s)) :
    ⊤ ≤ Submodule.span k
      ((⋃ j : ℕ, (fun z => t ^ j * z) '' s) ∪ (⋃ j : ℕ, (fun z => u ^ j * z) '' s)) := by
  have hut : u * t = 1 := by rw [mul_comm]; exact htu
  have hpow : ∀ n : ℕ, u ^ n * t ^ n = 1 := fun n => by
    rw [← mul_pow, hut, one_pow]
  intro m _
  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := H m
  -- `m = u ^ n * (t ^ n * m)`
  have hm : m = u ^ n * (t ^ n * m) := by
    rw [← mul_assoc, hpow, one_mul]
  -- push the span through the `k`-linear map `mul by u ^ n`
  have hmap := Submodule.map_span (LinearMap.mulLeft k (u ^ n))
    (⋃ j : ℕ, (fun z => t ^ j * z) '' s)
  have hmem : u ^ n * (t ^ n * m) ∈
      Submodule.span k ((fun z => u ^ n * z) '' ⋃ j : ℕ, (fun z => t ^ j * z) '' s) := by
    rw [← hmap]
    exact Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hn
  rw [← hm] at hmem
  refine Submodule.span_le.mpr ?_ hmem
  rintro _ ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
  simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_image] at hz
  obtain ⟨j, a, ha, rfl⟩ := hz
  rcases le_or_lt n j with hnj | hjn
  · -- `u^n * (t^j * a) = t^(j-n) * a`
    apply Submodule.subset_span
    refine Or.inl (Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨j - n, Set.mem_image_of_mem _ ha |>.imp ?_⟩)
    sorry
  · sorry

end TwoLattice

end AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic
